message.reasoning:
We need to produce JSON according to schema: kind, assistant_message, tool_call (if tool_call), acquired_fact_ids (array), readiness_state.

We are at frontier: need to expand_file via session.expand_file@v1 with target selector? The history shows they already executed session.expand_file and got status:ok. Now they want to continue? The objective: gather evidence needed before editing schema definitions, so need to get evidence of schema version constants. Likely need to hydrate source then expand file? Already done hydrate_source then expand_file. Now maybe next step is to read the expanded file content? Could use session.explain_export_omission or session.export to get content. Or use session.hydrate? Actually they need evidence: maybe need to "session.explain_export_omission" to see what will be exported? Or "session.export" to get full export. Or "session.read_file"? Not listed. Tools available: session.expand_file (done), session.hydrate_source (done), session.export (can export). To get evidence of schema version constants, perhaps need to "session.export" with appropriate options to capture definitions. Or "session.explain_export_omission" to see omitted symbols.

The frontier recommended action: hydrate_source [F1] relation=- direction=- candidates=0 priority=120 via session.hydrate(...). That was done. Next recommended: expand_file [F1] priority=112 candidates=17. That was done. Now next heuristic? maybe "session.export" to get exported symbols? Or "session.explain_export_omission" to see what will be omitted. Or "session.hydrate" again? The task: gather exact evidence needed before editing schema definitions so artifacts stay versioned and aligned. So we need to get the concrete values of schema version constants defined in artifacts.rs. Those are symbols like RUN_MANIFEST_SCHEMA_VERSION, CONTEXT_OBJECT_SCHEMA_VERSION, TASK_SPEC_SCHEMA_VERSION, TURN_TRACE_SCHEMA_VERSION, SCORE_REPORT_SCHEMA_VERSION, CONTEXT_WINDOW_SECTION_SCHEMA_VERSION. We need to capture their definitions (likely static constants). To get them, we can "session.export" with include_rendered true to get full source? Or "session.explain_export_omission" to see omitted? Perhaps "session.export" with visible_levels all? The tool contract: session.export payload includes max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, etc. So we can call session.export with include_rendered:true to get rendered source of the file, which would contain those constants.

Thus next action: tool_call session.export@v1 with payload maybe {"visible_levels": ["*"]} or something. The payload schema not fully specified but we can include fields. Probably need at least target_selector? The export tool payload description: optional max_tokens, compact, include_rendered, visible_levels, only_node_classes, exclude_node_classes, max_frontier_actions, max_rendered_bytes. No required fields. So we can just provide {"include_rendered":true}. Or we can specify "visible_levels":"*"? Might need to be a string? Not sure. Keep simple: {"include_rendered":true}. That should export the file content.

Thus produce a JSON with kind: tool_call, tool_call tool_name "session.export@v1", payload with include_rendered:true maybe. Also assistant_message brief status. acquired_fact_ids maybe empty now? Probably we haven't acquired any new fact ids yet. readiness_state maybe "not_ready" or "evidence_visible"? Since we are about to call export, we are not ready yet. So readiness_state: "not_ready". acquired_fact_ids: [] (maybe empty). Provide assistant_message: "Calling session.export to capture schema version constants". Provide tool_call payload.

Make sure JSON shape: {"kind":"tool_call","assistant_message":"<brief status>","tool_call":{"tool_name":"session.export@v1","payload":{...}},"acquired_fact_ids":[],"readiness_state":"not_ready"}.

No extra fields. Ensure no markdown. Provide exact JSON.

Let's craft assistant_message: "exporting artifacts.rs to capture schema version constants". Use short string.

Payload: maybe {"include_rendered":true}. That's valid JSON.

Thus final output.
